JCI Hits 7,128 at Open on Tax Incentive Hopes
body: JCI opens up 0.31% to 7,128, supported by tax incentive hopes and 8.5% FDI growth, as US–Iran tensions and oil surge cloud global outlook.

title: Jakarta Faces Retail Rice Shortage as Costs Surge
body: Rising costs and price caps squeeze margins, forcing suppliers to halt shipments and raising risks of rice shortages in retailers.

title: Indonesia Weighs Plastic Stimulus as Global Price Surge Hits Industry
body: Indonesia weighs stimulus and faster imports as global plastic prices surge, squeezing industries and risking higher consumer prices.

title: Minister Rejects Claims of Shrinking Indonesia Cash Buffer
body: Purbaya says Rp 420T SAL remains intact, dismissing depletion rumors and warning of tighter fiscal discipline under “survival mode.”

title: Finance Minister Signals Tax Incentives for Capital Markets
body: Indonesia is considering tax incentives, including income tax cuts and capital gains exemptions to boost deepen domestic capital markets.

title: Indonesia Resumes Shrimp Exports to US, Ships 3,400 Containers
body: Indonesia resumes shrimp exports to the US, shipping 3,400 containers worth Rp11 trillion after tightening safety standards.

title: JCI Reverses Gains, Closes Down 0.32% Near 7,100
body: JCI slipped 0.32% to 7,106 as early gains faded near key support, with external risks and oil concerns capping investor appetite.

title: Indonesia Less Dependent on Mid-East Oil Than Other Asian Nations: Minister
body: Indonesia’s limited reliance on Hormuz oil routes helps shield the economy from global energy shocks, Airlangga says.

title: BI Unveils 'Pinisi' to Drive Financing, Back 5.4% Growth Goal
body: Bank Indonesia launches Pinisi to boost financing and support growth as global uncertainty, capital outflows, and high rates persist.

title: Indonesia’s 50% Biodiesel Program Becomes Global Model, Energy Official Says
body: Indonesia’s world-first B50 biodiesel program is attracting global attention as trials expand from roads to railways.

title: Indonesia Economy Remains Stable, Recession Risk Below 5%: Airlangga
body: Airlangga says Indonesia’s economy remains stable, backed by steady growth, low inflation, strong consumption, and sustained trade surplus.

title: Beyond $320 Billion: Will ASEAN, EU Keep Trade Pact Goal Alive in Brunei?
body: ASEAN and the EU have previously entertaihned the idea of securing a bloc-to-bloc free trade agreement.

title: Bank Indonesia Vows to Keep Intervening in Markets Despite Criticism
body: Critics said that the central bank’s market intervention was akin to “salting the ocean” -- a futile effort that only wastes resources.

title: JCI Ticks Up 0.41%, Tracking Wall Street, Oil Risks Restrain Momentum
body: JCI rose 0.41% to 7,158 at the open, tracking Wall Street gains, but oil risks and Hormuz disruption kept sentiment cautious.

title: BREN, Indokripto Among Top Losers as IDX Loses $52 Billion After Brutal Week
body: Indonesian stocks lost $52 billion in value after a tough week, with the benchmark index falling 6.61%.

title: Toyota Fortuner, Mitsubishi Pajero Used in Indonesia’s 50% Biodiesel Trial
body: Indonesia is testing B50 biodiesel in SUVs, trucks, and buses ahead of a planned June mandate.

title: UK’s BII Launches $1.4 Billion Climate Fund to Tackle Asia Coal Reliance
body: BII launched a $1.4 billion climate initiative to mobilize private capital for Asia’s energy transition, targeting coal-reliant markets.

title: Indonesia Market Sinks 6.6% in a Week, $52 Billion Wiped Out
body: Indonesia’s stocks fell 6.6% in a week, wiping out $52 billion in value, as foreign outflows and global uncertainty weighed on sentiment.

title: Indonesia Enters ‘Survival Mode’ as Fiscal Pressures Intensify
body: Indonesia is entering a fiscal “survival mode” as rising oil prices and tight budget constraints force the government to tighten spending

title: Vale Indonesia Lands $750 Million Green Loan to Fund Nickel Expansion
body: Vale Indonesia secures a $750 million sustainability-linked loan, drawing strong demand as nickel demand for EV batteries surges globally.
